item.title: Improved Perception for Machine Vision Under Complex Illumination
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 01 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00104-5

Optical interference from lasers and intense lighting can confuse machine vision. Now, a tunable band-stop photodetector has been designed that uses machine learning to suppress disruptive spectral components at the sensor, enabling more reliable perception in challenging illumination.

item.title: Tunable Band-Stop Photodetection with Machine Learning-Enabled Broadband Spectral Adaptation
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 01 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00098-0

A machine learning-guided tunable band-stop photodetector enables adaptive suppression of unpredictable light interference for robust broadband machine vision.

item.title: AI Clones in Conversation at Academic Conferences
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 26 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00089-1

Artificial intelligence (AI) clones could serve as a new interface for scientific communication between publishers and research communities. At a Nature conference in Seoul, multimodal AI clones — including one of the Chief Editor of Nature Sensors — engaged researchers in real-time dialogue about publishing and careers, in three different languages.

item.title: Non-Contact Biomechanical Profiling of 3D Tissues
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 26 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00096-2

A wireless biomechanical sensing platform enables non-contact, scalable, cost-effective profiling of three-dimensional (3D) cardiac organoids and engineered heart tissues.

item.title: Wireless and Contactless Biomechanic Well Plate for Monitoring Cardiac Organoid and 3D-Tissue Contraction
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 26 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00087-3

A wireless and contactless biomechanical well plate leverages ultrasensitive silicon cantilevers to enable high-throughput, real-time quantification of contractile forces in cardiac organoids and engineered heart tissues.

item.title: Integrated Thermal Sensing of Vascular Morphology for Haemodynamic Monitoring
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00082-8

A wearable thermal sensing approach enables non-invasive inference of vascular depth and diameter from skin thermal transients, improving the accuracy of haemodynamic modelling.

item.title: In-Sensor Spectral Decoding for Efficient Spectroscopic Recognition
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00085-5

In-sensor spectral decoding enables direct spectral reconstruction and application-level recognition without off-chip computation, delivering efficient spectroscopic sensing.

item.title: Multiparametric Molecular Imaging of the Brain
item.description:
Nature Sensors, Published online: 09 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s44460-026-00088-2

Multiparametric molecular imaging of the brain
